sentences.text: Hello.

Good.
How are
you?

I have no.
Yeah, the week is moving along.
Well,
good things all around.
It sounds.

I'm in Madison, Wisconsin.
Ok.
Yeah, I've been to Wisconsin.
It is beautiful this time of year.
Um, but fall is quickly coming.
So, trying to prepare myself.
I love that.
I wish our weather would cool down a little bit so we can get there.
But I think we're probably still a month or so out before the temperatures start to drop.
Yeah.
Are you from San Diego?
I am.
Yeah.
Born and raised.
I love it here.
Nice.
I have loved it every time I visited.

So, very new to Cardinal.

Ok.
How's it going so far?
I really enjoy it.
Yeah, I was really excited to join kind of the overall culture of Cardinal team that I'm working with for this new DEV.

So, yeah, I feel really confident, I feel really good about it.
Excited for victory on Pao.

Um for pure education properties.

So literally same on Montezuma in S CS U area.
Um And it's funny because where we're building victory on Pao is right down the street from my first student housing community in 2018.
So it feels very full circle to do this project.
Yeah.
Yeah, it's got to be fun working at different properties around the same area and kind of getting like a feel for it.
Sure.
So this is a new build, you said
yes, new development it's scheduled for delivery uh August 2025.
Ok.
Awesome, cool.
Well, are you familiar with rentable?
Have you worked with us at any other roles that you've had?
I don't think so.
Um I know we've a few different um ad ad revenue or ad management, but um not familiar with rentable specifically.
Ok.
Yeah.
So um I wanted to jump on a call because we do have a couple of different product offerings.
Um So I wanted to see what would make the most sense uh with you guys.
So it sounds like from the email that I got.
It sounds like you were inquiring about the rentable advertising.
Um So we have two different sides.
So one is we have a rental platform that syndicates out to a bunch of different ILSS and we do targeted social ads um as well.
And then we have another side that is Google Business Automation.
So we're um posting pricing and availability and posts over to your Google Business profiles and social pages.
So I guess just out of that quick rundown, which are you think your DRS address for them?
Sure.
It sounds like the first option would be more of our ballpark just because it's uh secondary with the integration that we already have in intrata.
And uh it sounds like we already have some of those bases covered.
So yeah, sounds like maybe the first option.
Ok, that sounds good.
So it sounds like you're in a spot where you're because it's a lease up that you're just trying to get your units full right now.
Yeah, exactly.
Um We went live quote unquote last week on the 14th.
Um but the students don't come back until the 26th.
So we're really just trying to make sure that we get our name out there, we're building out our brand on social media.
Um But yeah, for sure as many leads as we can get as possible, I think is the the big thing.
So as many eyes, as many um kind of sources of traffic, I guess you could say.
Ok.
Ok.
Well, I will jump in and I'll show you um the rentable platform, let me know if you can see that.
Yeah.
Ok.

Um So rental platform itself, um we a little bit different than like a standard Ils um where we're, we're really trying to focus away from like old marketing plans where say your property did most of their leasing and spring you would just come on for spring leasing and then stop advertising until the next year.
Really, the new way that we are building this advertising is to get exposure across multiple different platforms and different sites and to have consistency throughout the year.
So we do this in a few different ways.
Um So I told you a little bit about this, but we have the rental platform.
Um So you would be on the rental platform itself, but then how we really differentiate ourselves is through our network.
So we're able to syndicate your your listings out to about 80% of renters searching across all Ils the entire ecosystem of ilss.
So you get your, your listing on rentable and then we send it out to all of these other sites.
Uh The couple that you don't see in here one being the biggest one.
We don't syndicate out there, but about 80% of Ils as we do.
So it's a really great way to get consistent exposure and make sure that your properties have listings on all of these sites through one source.
OK?
The last piece of this is the targeted social ads.
Um You've mentioned social.
What are you guys doing on social right
now?
We literally just created our accounts like maybe a week ago.
So we have Tik Tok Instagram and Facebook right now.
OK.
So these, we, we do a couple of different things on social, but this one here that's included in our rental package.
This is targeted social ads.
So this kind of goes back to you saying the focus right now is the ads getting as many eyes, getting as many leads as we can.
This is a great way to do that.
Um So we we send your your listings from the rentable list, the one sorry, the rentable site to Facebook and Instagram, but these are sponsored ads.
So these are coming up like when you're scrolling through your news feeds and you see like a Nike ad, that's where these are coming up.
So you don't have to have followers.
You don't have to have people that you're connected with.
We're running all of these as sponsored paid ads and then all that lead traffic is just coming back through your lead email.
Yeah, that sounds awesome.
Ok,
perfect.
Uh The last piece of this is we do have uh an A I tool in the background.
That's, that's powering all of this.
So we can really see property by property which of these sources is performing the best.
So you say you have and I know we're just talking one property right now, but if we were in the future talking about more, we can see property by property.
Say Instagram is getting a ton of leads for this property, but maybe realtor.com is getting more traffic here.
This A I tool can see which of those platforms are performing best um and push more ads that way.
So take more of that spend per property and push it towards those ads.
Ok.
Yeah, perfect.
Yeah,
cool.
Well, any any questions on any of this?
Um I don't think I have any questions about the function.
I almost now feel like now that I've seen um the logo that it does seem a little bit familiar.
I'm not sure if I ever actually entered into a contract with rentable in the past, but all the functions that I mentioned.
Yeah, definitely sound familiar.

Um I think as long as like everything works out with our budget like again because we technically already went live, we're definitely wanting to get pen to paper sooner than later when it comes to these contracts.
So I guess Kylie, the same team that we um are on the email chain with if we can maybe circulate costs, um that would be the next best thing.
But yeah, in my opinion, just from the two options that you had mentioned, it sounds like the first like what we just went over is the best thing.
Um if I could just ask, I want to make sure once we do decide on this, if we ever say like, you know what?
We actually need a little bit more support, we need a little bit more effort.
Is that something we can upgrade to in the future?
Yeah, in terms of looking at the other, the other offering or in terms of getting more.
Yeah, yeah,
we can.
So there's, yeah, there's two different options.
So one of the the pricing I you you mentioned that.
So our pricing is 199 per property per month.
Um That includes everything we've gone over today.
So the rental platform, the I OS Partnership Network and then our paid social.
Um So it's 199 per property per month here.
And then yeah, if you did have interest um in looking at the Google side of it as well.
Um This is a great way to get exposure across Google.
Um get more eyes on your Google business profile and we do have a tool in here that automates posts to those social pages that you just created.
Ok.
So almost ok.
I almost like there might be something that we have like that already.
So that's the only reason I'm mentioning that, but the 199 that you mentioned, I definitely um it fits within our budget.
So yeah, um whatever we need to do to kind of get the next steps going.
Um the platform itself seems pretty user friendly.
But right now at the site level, it would be myself and then we're looking to get a leasing and marketing team leader who joins us.
And then I think we'd be the ones mainly using the platform every day.
OK.
Yeah, that sounds good.
It um implementation is pretty simple because you have, you guys have intrata.
Um So we turn on the integration with intrata and as soon as that integration is turned on really, the listing builds itself and then it's our marketing team that's doing the the postings here.
So you don't have to create anything or do anything.
Um You will have log in to the back end if you wanted to ever go in and make any changes to your listing for whatever reason, you can also see lead performance back there.
Um But I would also, I would also be doing check ins with you guys too, so we'll continue meeting um going over lead performance and making sure everything looks good.
Lovely, wonderful.
Well, thank you so much.
This was really, really helpful, really easy to understand.
Yeah,
yeah, I know sometimes it's like we get on and it's, it's a short conversation, but I wanna make sure that we're talking about the right product before I just send over a contract.
So,
yeah.
No, for sure.
And I know with everyone being in a bunch of different places, we're trying to take as much ownership over some of the things that we can.
So yeah, being familiar with that market, um with this market, I should say, I definitely feel like this is something we're gonna want to do.
So I think that Lauren is still signing our contracts or it might be Karissa, so still whatever next steps we need to do if we can keep that same body of people on the email.

Um But yeah, to me, it sounds like definitely something we want as far as being able to get our name out there and being able to, like you said, have some really seamless, um, posting going on with the sponsor and stuff.
I think that's gonna really help us.

I'll reiterate the pricing of 199.
I'll see.
I'll double check that it's to send a contract through.

Well, thanks for meeting with me, Kylie.

Of course.

Ok.
